# Rehabilitation / Recovery Centre Site — Nigeria-First Research Brief

**Niche ID:** P3-rehab-centre-rehab-centre-site
**Vertical slug:** rehab-centre
**VN-ID:** VN-HLT-001
**Niche family:** NF-HLT-RHB (standalone)
**Family role:** standalone
**Research date:** 2026-04-26

## 1. Nigerian Business Reality
Nigeria's rehabilitation and addiction recovery sector is regulated by NDLEA (National Drug Law Enforcement Agency) and supervised by the Federal Ministry of Health. Facilities range from government-run NDLEA rehabilitation centres to private faith-based and clinical centres across Lagos, Abuja, and Ibadan. Stigma is significant — confidentiality is the #1 family concern. WhatsApp is the preferred first-contact channel (anonymous enquiry). Cost ranges from ₦150,000 to ₦2M+ per 90-day residential programme.

## 2. Target Audience Profile
- **Desperate parent Mrs Adaeze, 48, Lagos**: Has a son with substance dependency. Urgently needs a confidential, trusted facility. WhatsApps for immediate help.
- **Worried spouse Tunde, 35, Abuja**: Partner has alcohol dependency. Needs to understand treatment protocols and costs before committing. Values clinical credibility.
- **HR director Ngozi, 42, PH**: Employee assistance programme referral. Needs a facility with medical supervision, aftercare, and corporate billing.

## 3. Recommended Website Structure
- **Home**: Hero (compassionate, confidential, hope-forward), WhatsApp helpline CTA, programmes summary, NDLEA/FMOH trust strip, discreet enquiry option
- **About**: Centre story, clinical director profile, MDCN-licensed practitioners, NDLEA licence, facility description
- **Services (Programmes)**: Residential detox, outpatient, aftercare, family counselling — each with ₦ price range and duration
- **Contact**: WhatsApp confidential helpline (prominently displayed), discreet enquiry form (NDPR-compliant), location (vague for client safety)

## 4. Content Tone & Voice
Compassionate, hope-affirming, clinically credible. "Confidential & Caring", "NDLEA Licensed", "Recovery is Possible" language. Never stigmatising language. No shock imagery. Primary CTA: "Call / WhatsApp Our Helpline". Secondary: "Enquire Confidentially".

## 5. Trust Signal Inventory
- NDLEA rehabilitation centre licence number
- FMOH approval
- MDCN-licensed clinical director
- CAC registration
- Confidentiality promise statement
- Aftercare and family support programme
- Faith-based affiliation (where applicable)

## 6. Image Direction (5-7 Specific Concepts)
1. Group therapy session — circle of chairs, warm lighting, supportive setting (no faces showing)
2. Garden/outdoor space at facility — peaceful, green, recovery environment
3. Clinical consultation — doctor and patient (discreet, no identifiable faces)
4. Family reunion — dignified, hopeful, recovery milestone
5. Facility exterior — welcoming, non-institutional, clean signage
6. Counsellor one-on-one — supportive posture, private setting
7. Aftercare group — former clients giving back, community support

## 7. Regulatory Compliance Checklist
- [ ] NDLEA rehabilitation centre licence displayed
- [ ] FMOH supervision noted
- [ ] MDCN-licensed practitioners named
- [ ] CAC registration
- [ ] Confidentiality and NDPR policy for health data
- [ ] Crisis helpline number (SURPIN: 0800-9773583) referenced

## 8. Nigeria-First Compliance Checklist
- [x] WhatsApp confidential helpline CTA prominent
- [x] NDLEA licence trust signal
- [x] ₦ programme pricing
- [x] Nigerian facility context (Lagos, Abuja, Ibadan)
- [x] Confidentiality first approach
- [x] Mobile-first layout
- [x] esc() on all inputs
- [x] NDPR health data consent on form
